scope: Frontier vision and BEV perception techniques most relevant to TSQBEV's teacher/student reset, with direct notes on how to apply them in multimodal autonomous driving.
source_policy: Primary sources only: official paper pages, official code repositories, and official checkpoint/model pages.
design_laws: Use the strongest unrestricted teacher available, but keep the student's projection and fusion path regular enough to distill and deploy.
Foundation backbones help only when the downstream bridge is adapted to geometry, depth, and perspective rather than used as raw frozen image tokens.
Shared BEV or sparse world-state representations must be supervised where the task bottleneck lives: geometry, quality ranking, region occupancy, or vector structure.

entries.title: DINOv3
entries.core_trick: Train a large-scale self-supervised vision backbone whose intermediate features remain strong for dense downstream transfer without task-specific pretraining.
entries.intuition: A strong camera teacher should give the student semantically richer multi-view evidence before BEV fusion, but only if the projector preserves geometry-relevant structure instead of flattening everything into generic classification features.
ablation_takeaways: The gain comes from dense transferable features, not from blindly replacing every downstream block with a huge ViT.
Projection and downstream adaptation matter; frozen foundation features without a perspective/BEV bridge often underperform their apparent pretraining strength.
ablation_nuggets: Prefer linear or light adapter projection into the student camera branch before BEV or sparse-world fusion.
Start with frozen or lightly adapted features; only unfreeze after the bridge and supervision are stable.
apply_when: camera semantics and long-tail recognition are limiting the student more than raw LiDAR geometry
the teacher is unconstrained and can afford heavier image computation than the deployable student
avoid_when: the camera branch is already bandwidth-bound and no projection/adaptation budget is available
you cannot supervise the geometry bridge and expect generic image features to solve 3D localization alone
entries.integration_pattern: Use as frozen or lightly adapted multi-view camera feature extractor, then project into the BEV/sparse fusion interface and distill teacher quality or BEV signals into the student.
failure_modes: feature richness that does not translate into better 3D boxes because the perspective-to-world bridge is weak
GPU memory blow-up if the projector and view stack are not checkpointed
entries.student_role: lightly adapted camera feature source or teacher-only backbone
tsqbev_actions: project DINOv3 multi-view features into the camera branch before BEV/sparse fusion
pair with perspective supervision or BEV-space distillation rather than raw feature concatenation

entries.title: SAM 2.1
entries.core_trick: Use a strong memory-conditioned segmentation model to turn boxes, prompts, or sparse hints into dense object or region support that can supervise proposal priors and foreground structure.
entries.intuition: SAM 2.1 is valuable here as a dense region prior generator and teacher, not as a direct replacement for BEV detection. Its best use is to preserve object support and boundaries that our student ranking and region losses currently lose.
ablation_takeaways: Dense priors are most useful when they regularize object support, occupancy, or foreground regions around teacher hypotheses.
The model is too heavy and too generic to be treated as the deployable perception trunk.
ablation_nuggets: Use proposal boxes or teacher boxes to prompt SAM and extract masks or support maps.
Supervise region objectness or occupancy rather than trying to backpropagate through a full SAM runtime in the student loop.
apply_when: ranking and object support are the bottleneck and box-only supervision is too sparse
the teacher can afford heavy dense region inference offline or asynchronously
avoid_when: you need the final deployable student to run a full SAM stack online
the issue is raw geometry calibration rather than region support
entries.integration_pattern: Generate region priors or support masks from teacher proposals and use them for offline cache, region-objectness targets, or proposal filtering.
failure_modes: masks that look good in image space but are not consistent across multi-view geometry
high preprocessing cost if every sample requires full dense prompting online
entries.teacher_role: dense region and mask prior teacher
tsqbev_actions: turn teacher boxes into SAM 2.1 region priors cached alongside detection samples
use region priors for teacher-region objectness and occupancy losses, not as a direct runtime trunk

entries.title: BEVFormer v2
entries.core_trick: Adapt strong image backbones to BEV recognition by supervising them in the native perspective space before or alongside BEV aggregation.
entries.intuition: Camera foundation models are trained in image space. Perspective supervision reduces the gap between strong 2D semantics and the geometry-sensitive BEV task by explicitly teaching the bridge where the 3D cues live.
ablation_takeaways: Perspective-space supervision is one of the highest-ROI additions when a stronger image backbone does not automatically improve BEV quality.
The gain is larger when the backbone is strong but the BEV bridge is the actual bottleneck.
ablation_nuggets: Add perspective losses before expecting BEV metrics to reflect the extra image backbone capacity.
Use it to stabilize DINOv3 projection rather than as a standalone architecture switch.
apply_when: camera backbone quality is strong but BEV 3D precision remains weak
multi-view sparse attention needs a geometry-aware training signal
avoid_when: the main bottleneck is LiDAR box quality or export calibration rather than image-to-world adaptation
the student cannot afford any additional image-space supervision budget
entries.integration_pattern: Attach perspective losses to projected multi-view foundation features before BEV/sparse-world fusion.
failure_modes: extra image-space loss that helps 2D features but not 3D metrics because the fusion head is still bottlenecked
training instability if perspective and BEV losses are not balanced
tsqbev_actions: add perspective supervision to the DINOv3 camera branch before sparse/BEV fusion
treat it as the main adaptation mechanism for frontier image backbones

entries.title: Sparse4D
entries.core_trick: Use sparse temporally propagated object/world tokens instead of dense BEV everywhere, while keeping temporal memory and sparse attention centered on likely objects.
entries.intuition: This is the right student family when dense BEV is too expensive for deployment, but only if the sparse tokens inherit enough teacher quality and geometric context.
ablation_takeaways: Sparse tokens win when they preserve the right objects; they fail when ranking and source-mix collapse destroy recall.
Temporal carry and sparse attention are useful only after the token set itself is reliable.
ablation_nuggets: Attach stronger teacher-quality and region supervision before iterating on token routing details.
Use official-metric checkpointing, not just loss, because sparse failures can hide behind plausible training curves.
apply_when: student deployment constraints rule out dense BEV everywhere
the teacher can provide high-quality sparse targets or BEV-space distillation signals
avoid_when: the token proposals are still untrustworthy and no teacher signal preserves them
you are trying to use sparse queries as a substitute for missing camera geometry adaptation
entries.integration_pattern: Keep Sparse4D-style student state, but supervise it with DINOv3+perspective and teacher BEV/quality priors.
failure_modes: source-mix collapse to one modality or proposal family
token budget optimizations that reduce recall faster than they improve latency
tsqbev_actions: keep sparse temporal student as the deployable target while upgrading its supervision and camera features
stop local token tweaks unless official metrics improve against the v28 control

entries.title: DistillBEV
entries.core_trick: Distill strong LiDAR teacher knowledge into a camera BEV student using BEV-aligned supervision instead of only box-level labels.
entries.intuition: When the camera student has semantics but weak geometry, a LiDAR teacher can teach it the BEV structure directly, reducing the need to rediscover geometry from sparse labels.
ablation_takeaways: BEV-space supervision is materially stronger than box-only supervision when modality gaps are large.
Teacher signal must target the student's bottleneck representation, not just final boxes.
ablation_nuggets: Use teacher BEV or region maps when box/ranking supervision alone plateaus.
Do not pay the cost unless the student actually exposes a BEV-aligned or world-aligned supervision interface.
apply_when: camera branch quality is limited by missing geometric supervision
a strong LiDAR teacher is already available
avoid_when: the student has no stable BEV/world representation to receive the distillation signal
teacher and student coordinate systems are mismatched or noisy
entries.integration_pattern: Distill from unrestricted LiDAR or multimodal teacher into BEV/world-aligned student features or quality maps.
failure_modes: teacher maps that are strong but geometrically misaligned with the student raster or token space
teacher supervision dominating weak student optimization and causing collapse
entries.student_role: camera or sparse student supervision target
tsqbev_actions: add BEV/world-space teacher supervision if DINOv3 plus ranking fixes still trail the v28 control
prioritize distilling quality maps and region occupancy before more local sparse-token tuning

entries.title: UniDistill
entries.core_trick: Use a unified BEV-centric distillation framework so multiple teacher modalities can supervise the same downstream 3D student.
entries.intuition: The important lesson is not just that distillation helps; it is that a BEV/world-aligned teacher space can unify supervision across camera, LiDAR, and multimodal teachers.
ablation_takeaways: Cross-modality distillation lifts both mAP and NDS when the teacher and student align in BEV.
The student benefits most when the distillation target is universal and not over-specialized to one upstream architecture.
ablation_nuggets: Use UniDistill-style targets when multiple teachers should supervise one student interface.
Prefer world/BEV targets over architecture-specific hidden-state mimicry.
apply_when: teacher family diversity is a strength and you want one student to absorb it
you need one distillation interface that survives teacher swaps
avoid_when: the student has no shared BEV/world target space
teacher signals disagree badly and you have no quality filter
entries.integration_pattern: Distill strong LiDAR and multimodal teachers into one BEV/sparse student interface with teacher-quality filtering.
failure_modes: hidden-state distillation that overfits to teacher architecture quirks instead of the task
tsqbev_actions: treat UniDistill as the template if we use CenterPoint, BEVFusion, and SAM-derived priors together
distill teacher consensus into one student BEV/world supervision space

entries.title: MapTRv2
entries.core_trick: Represent lane and map outputs as vectorized elements on top of a shared BEV representation rather than as only raster segmentation.
entries.intuition: Lane learning should not just be another raster loss pulling on detection. A vector head isolates the map task better and gives more controllable multitask boundaries.
ablation_takeaways: Vector-map supervision is better aligned with downstream map structure than raw dense lane masks alone.
Shared BEV helps only when task boundaries and checkpoint selection are explicit.
ablation_nuggets: Start lane learning on a frozen or lightly adapted shared trunk before full joint finetuning.
Use vector metrics and detection non-regression gates together.
apply_when: lane integration is important but naive raster multitask training is destabilizing detection
the shared representation is already world-aligned
avoid_when: the trunk is too unstable to share across detection and lane tasks
you only have weak raster labels and no usable vector supervision
entries.integration_pattern: Stage lane integration with a MapTRv2-style head over a stable detection trunk or shared BEV/student world state.
failure_modes: multitask interference when lane loss drives trunk drift faster than checkpointing can detect it
lane improvements that hide a broken detection export
tsqbev_actions: replace naive lane-only shared-loss coupling with staged MapTRv2-style vector supervision
only promote joint checkpoints when detection stays inside a strict non-regression band
